title: Leadership | Raleigh Hills Business Association
description: Mary Sherman, Communication, Lead Sherman Family Properties Having lived and worked in the Raleigh Hills area for most of my life, I am happy to serve on the Raleigh Hills Business Association leadership team. It is always great to be part of an organization dedicated to improving the area, benefiting school children and supporting neighbors
htmlMain:
Mary Sherman, Communication, Lead

Sherman Family Properties

Having lived and worked in the Raleigh Hills area for most of my life, I am happy to serve on the Raleigh Hills Business Association leadership team. It is always great to be part of an organization dedicated to improving the area, benefiting school children and supporting neighbors and the business community. I am glad to have met a dedicated group of hard working people, and enjoy supporting their businesses. Increasing livability of our neighborhood is very important to me and am glad it is part of our mission statement.

Our family, woman owned, business offers Office Space in the Raleigh Hills area. We like serving small locally owned businesses and would be happy to find a spot to meet the needs your business! We have a number of office options east of 217, between Canyon Road and Beaverton Hillsdale Hwy.

Contact Mary at Sherman@ShermanFamilyProperties.com or 503-805-7747 for more information.

Mark FitzMaurice, Assistant Lead

Episcopal Parish of St. John the Baptist

Mark FitzMaurice is treasurer and head of the finance committee for the Episcopal Parish of St. John the Baptist, on the campus of Oregon Episcopal School. He also helps organize St. John’s participation in community efforts to address homelessness, affordable housing and incarceration.

Mark has prior experience as the owner of a computer consulting business. He spent most of his professional career managing technical professionals developing technology products for large multinational companies.

St. John’s values their connection through RHBA to the local business community. Many of our members live, shop and work in Raleigh Hills, and we look for opportunities to serve local needs.

Contact Mark at fitzmaur1231@gmail.com or 503.245.3777.

Gabrielle Proust, Secretary

The Efficiency Godmother

Gabrielle is a Daily Money Manager and Efficiency Specialist who joyously transforms chaos into smooth, effective processes for individuals and businesses so they can work more efficiently and bring harmony to life.

With more than 25 years in organizing data, files and supplies, identifying process improvements, accounting and reconciliation, training and mentoring in operations protocols, and HR recruitment and on-boarding, Gabrielle is qualified as an expert. As a member of NAPO, NAPO-Oregon and AADMM, she keeps current in learning and implementing software, phone applications and best practices for efficiency.

Gabrielle has served on a number of non-profit boards since 1995, including: North Coast Co-op, 5 years; PRO referral organization, 3 years; Toastmasters District 7, 2 years; NAPO-Oregon, 4 years; and the Jewish Community Orchestra, 10+ years.  She is an added compliment to our leadership.

Contact Gabrielle at Gabrielle@efficiencygodmother.com 971-238-2305

Bob Schmidt Treasurer

Groves Capital

Bob Schmidt is a dedicated mortgage financing expert with a passion for helping individuals and families achieve their dreams of home ownership. Bob takes the time to understand each client’s needs and works diligently to navigate the complexities of mortgage financing, ensuring a smooth and transparent process.

Prior to working in the mortgage industry, Bob provided sales coverage to large financial institutions in London, helping them to invest in the fixed income capital markets. Outside of work, Bob enjoys cycling, family, Arsenal and the Green Bay Packers.

Michael Chase, Communication

M F Chase Websites

When you need website work, you don’t need a geek or a friend…You need a Professional!

With over 20 years of website and marketing experience, my goal is to make website technology and integrated marketing a positive asset for the small business at an affordable price. By using the 7 principles of GPS Marketing we can make your website a tool rather than a static billboard  or an expense item. Every business needs a website today. The real question is: Should you be working for your website or should it be working for you?

Michael is responsible for technical development of the RHBA website and the Social media postings as well as the RHBA update. The monthly email that lets us all know what is going on and when, usually originates with him but depends on the input from other board members and the membership at large.

Contact Michael at michael@mfchase.com or 503-808-0021.

Bev Snow-Kuehn, Founding Board Member & Volunteer

B-Line Enterprises, LLC

Printing - Specialty Advertising “Merch/SWAG” and Print Broker = Solutions

Providing a mix of creative elements for YOUR NAME & YOUR BRAND

I have been in business since 1996. My clients are from a variety of professions: Propriety Schools, Colleges, Hospitals, US Government, Automotive Repair, Team Sports, Apartment Complexes, Environmental Reclamation, CPA's, HVAC’s, Plumbing, Neighbor Fairs, Non-profit companies, Computer repair & technology, Funeral/Cemetery services, Healing practices to name a few.

My goals are to provide you, my client, professional services. Providing you with cost, money and time savings for your next project or event. I do all the work to make you, and your event look good and be successful.

Keeping things fun and light. Serving you with a warm smile, the ability to impact your audience with ease in a loving caring atmosphere. Elevating your brand, one impression at a time!

I find the right supplier to imprint on most anything & everything. Small minimums welcome. Going green? Ask about product availability.

BANNERS / BUSINESS CARDS / STICKY NOTES / INTERIOR & EXTERIOR SIGNAGE / IDENTITY APPAREL / EMBROIDERY / SCREEN PRINTING, ETC. / MERCH/SWAG

Contact: Bev at BLineBev@gmail.com 503-452-0071

Melinda Wheeler  L.Ac.

Dragon Rises Center for Wholeness

Melinda Wheeler is a licensed acupuncturist and practitioner of Chinese medicine. With a deep understanding of classical acupuncture, she integrates both traditional and modern approaches to health and wellness. Melinda is dedicated to supporting her patient's holistic well-being through individualized treatments, blending her extensive knowledge of Chinese medicine with a compassionate and client-centered approach. She loves serving as a board member for the Raleigh Hills Business Association (RHBA), where she contributes to the growth and development of the local business community.

Joyce Allegra del Rosario

Combined Insurance, A CHUBB Company

Joyce Allegra del Rosario, an independent insurance agent, a strategic consulting for service-minded teams. She brings a wide variety of skills to our Business Association. CERT Certified, Southwest Hills  Residential League, Inc., Sons & Daughters of Oregon Pioneers, Community Volunteer with Minority Veterans of America.

Toni Curry, Founding Member & Ex Officio Member
